### Post by mental_drummer on 2006-06-01
Hi,
Ive just installed dapper and was followinf the how to on installing the nVidia drivers. I opened up xorg.conf to manually enable nvidia-glx but the text im looking to change isnt there

This is my xorg.conf file

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#

# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"

EndSection

EndSection

  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82845G/GL[Brookdale-G]/GE Chipset Integrated Graphics Device"
	Driver		"vesa"
	BusID		"PCI:2:9:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"NEC FE770"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Intel Corporation 82845G/GL[Brookdale-G]/GE Chipset Integrated Graphics Device"

	DefaultDepth	24

		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection

		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection



I'm not sure but it looks as if the graphics card isnt being used at all. When i installed dapper (and previously with breezy) Xwindows wouldnt start. I looked in a log file and cahnged the address to 
BusID		"PCI:2:9:0"
and it worked.

So i guess i need to find the correct address for my card but im not sure how to do it?

Any ideas - thanks in advance

---

### Post by johnc4510 on 2006-06-01
To start with instead of manually enabling the driver, try this:
sudo nvidia-glx-config enable
That should get x up and running and then you can go in and manually configure the card.

---

### Post by mental_drummer on 2006-06-01
Unfortunatley the reason im doing it manually is cos i tried that and i get this message.

Error: your X configuration has been altered.
This script cannot proceed automatically. If you believe that this
not correct, you can update the md5sum entry executing the following
command:
md5sum /etc/X11/xorg.conf | sudo tee /var/lib/x11/xorg.conf.md5sum
otherwise edit manually /etc/X11/xorg.conf to change the Driver section
from nv to nvidia.

thanks for the quick reply

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-06-01
Does this change anything?
```
sudo nvidia-xconfig
```

---

### Post by mental_drummer on 2006-06-02
cheers - that seems to have worked - I get the splash screen now when i start it up. ah you make it seem so easy!

---
